commit.message:
Fix ambient compensation: aggressive non-linear ramp for cold rooms

The controller was using a timid 0.5°F/degree linear compensation that was
also gated behind ML confidence < 0.3, meaning it was completely disabled
for phases where ML had enough training data. This meant in a 61°F room,
the controller would set -7 (active cooling) when the user needed +2
(heating).

Changes:
- Increase base compensation to 0.8°F/degree (was 0.5)
- Add extra 0.5°F/degree ramp below 65°F (non-linear, steeper in cold)
- Remove ML confidence gate — room temp is a physical reality, not a
  preference the ML can override. 61°F air always feels colder than 70°F.
- Add hard floor: below 60°F room, never set below neutral (0)
- Add logging for ambient compensation decisions

Result at 61°F room:
  Deep phase: -7 + 9 = +2 (heating) instead of -3 (cooling)
  REM phase:  -5 + 9 = +4 (heating) instead of -1 (cooling)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
初期コミット: ECサイト設計書（画面5件・バッチ2件）

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Replace Fisher's exact test with junction origin classification (#6)

* Switch test data to matched gastric cancer tumor/normal pair

Replace paired-end ERR188273 (GEUVADIS LCL) with single-end matched
gastric cancer samples from ENA:
  - SRR9143066: Primary Tumor (gastric cancer surgical section)
  - SRR9143065: Solid Tissue Normal (adjacent stomach tissue)

Both samples are freely available via ENA HTTPS. Single-end layout is
handled by the existing HISAT2 rule (empty fastq2 → -U mode).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Refactor filter_junctions: provenance-based classification

Replace per-sample novelty filtering with cross-sample classification.
Unannotated tumor junctions are now labeled by provenance:
  - tumor_specific:   absent in matched normal → neoepitope candidates
  - patient_specific: also present in normal   → excluded downstream

Normal samples are consumed internally to build an exclusion set and do
not appear in the output TSV. When no normal sample is present, all
unannotated junctions are labeled tumor_specific with a warning.

Adds min_normal_reads param (default: 2) to filter.smk and config.yaml
to avoid false patient_specific labels from single-read noise in normal.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Filter assemble_contigs to tumor_specific junctions only

patient_specific junctions (present in matched normal) are now excluded
from contig assembly and all downstream neoepitope prediction steps.
They remain in the novel_junctions.tsv for provenance reporting.

Gracefully skips the filter when junction_origin column is absent
(backwards-compatible with older junction TSVs).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Simplify reporting: replace statistical analysis with provenance summary

Remove statistical_analysis and summarise_all rules (and their dependency
on Fisher's exact test). Replace generate_report with a lightweight HTML
report showing:
  - junction provenance counts (tumor_specific vs patient_specific)
  - binder class summary (strong / weak / non)
  - top strong binders table

The summary_table.tsv target is removed from rule all; the per-run
report.html is sufficient for result verification.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove cancer_types_with_normal config; add min_normal_reads to test config

cancer_types_with_normal was only used by the now-removed statistical_analysis
rule. Normal sample handling is now implicit: any sample with 'Normal' in its
sample_type is used as the exclusion set in filter_junctions.

min_normal_reads (default: 2) is now explicit in test_config.yaml to match
the production config.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Replace 'provenance' with 'origin' throughout codebase

'Origin' is the standard term in cancer genomics for describing where
a junction comes from. 'Provenance' is a data-management term that does
not belong in biological code.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Trim CLAUDE.md: keep decisions/gotchas, remove change log

Remove the History section and dependency issue #4 (statistical_analysis,
now deleted). Add junction origin classification design rationale and
update test dataset to the gastric cancer matched pair.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add pipeline overview diagram and improve junction origin description

Add a CSS/HTML pipeline diagram showing the full workflow with the
tumor_specific / patient_specific branch clearly visualised.

Replace the vague "unannotated junctions classified by comparison against
the matched normal sample" with a plain-language description explaining
what patient_specific and tumor_specific mean biologically.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update README to reflect junction origin classification refactor

- Pipeline overview: replace Fisher's test step with origin classification
- Scientific background: remove statistical enrichment description
- sample_type description: remove Fisher's test reference
- Local testing: update to gastric cancer test pair (SRR9143066/SRR9143065)
  and actual expected output numbers
- Output description: remove statistics.tsv and summary_table.tsv, add
  junction_origin column note to novel_junctions.tsv
- Configuration table: add filtering.min_normal_reads
- Project structure: remove statistical_analysis.py

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove unused sys import from filter_junctions.py

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Jin-HoMLee/splice-neoepitope-pipeline/sessions/af383e10-d46a-4b07-ade3-ecde0f910c1c

Co-authored-by: Jin-HoMLee <123657753+Jin-HoMLee@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove dead filtering.strategy config parameter

The mean read-count filter in classify_junctions was hard-coded, making
filtering.strategy in the config misleading and unused. Removed it from
config.yaml, test_config.yaml, filter.smk params, and the README table.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Document auto_stop.sh as default run command in cloud guide

Replaced standalone snakemake command with the auto-stop version to
prevent idle VM billing. Added a warning callout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
[Build] Prefer Ninja over Make in build.sh (#364)

* [Build] Prefer Ninja over Make in build.sh

Ninja provides significantly better build performance for MLIR-based
projects compared to Unix Makefiles:

- Faster incremental builds: Ninja's dependency graph resolution is
  more efficient, which matters for projects with heavy TableGen
  (.td -> .inc) codegen pipelines.
- Better parallelism: Ninja uses a global dependency graph with global
  parallel scheduling, avoiding the serialization bottleneck of Make's
  recursive subdirectory traversal.
- More precise rebuilds: Ninja avoids unnecessary rebuilds caused by
  timestamp-based staleness checks on generated .inc files.
- LLVM/MLIR recommended: Ninja is the officially recommended generator
  for LLVM/MLIR projects and is used in upstream CI.

Falls back to Unix Makefiles when ninja is not installed.

Made-with: Cursor

* [Build] Reuse cached CMake generator to avoid mismatch errors

When a CMakeCache.txt already exists in the build directory, read the
cached CMAKE_GENERATOR and reuse it instead of re-detecting. This
prevents CMake from erroring out when the environment changes (e.g.
ninja installed/uninstalled after initial configure).

Fresh builds still auto-detect Ninja when available.

Made-with: Cursor

* Update scripts/build.sh

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use FormData + & encoding for Mailgun click tracking on all links

- Switch sendViaMailgun from URLSearchParams to FormData (multipart/form-data)
  to avoid URL-encoding overhead that caused partial click-tracking rewriting
- Encode & as & in newsletter personalized URLs for proper HTML href syntax
- Forward custom email headers (List-Unsubscribe, etc.) via h: prefix to Mailgun
- Root cause: Mailgun only rewrote 7/24 links with URLSearchParams; all CTA/footer
  links got tracking but content links (jobs, tools, articles) were skipped

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(demo): add --demo-auto flag for self-animating TUI

Adds auto-animation to demo mode for recording GIFs showing
tmux display-popup modals without external input.

Key changes:
- Add --demo-auto CLI flag (implies --demo)
- Implement tick_demo_auto() with timed synthetic navigation
- Add move_cursor_down/up helpers for view-agnostic movement
- Rewrite record-demo.sh to use self-animating popups
- Update demo.tape VHS recording

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: memory — mark all P0 DB cleanup items done

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(watcher): add --name flag for human-readable watcher IDs

Custom watchers were identified only by type+PID (e.g. 'custom-81271'),
making them hard to identify. The --name flag allows setting a readable
name like 'pilot watch custom --name gemma4-download --script ...'.

Key changes:
- Add --name CLI option to 'pilot watch' command
- Use name as watcher ID when provided, fall back to type-PID
- Pass --name through when spawning background process
- Pass watcher_id to all watch functions instead of hard-coding
- Update help text with --name usage

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: avoid duplicate runs on PR branches (#12979)

Previously both 'push' and 'pull_request' triggers fired for every commit
on a PR branch, causing two concurrent check runs. Restrict 'push' to
master and release branches only — these are the post-merge confirmation
runs. PRs are covered exclusively by the 'pull_request' trigger.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor(neo4j): reduce raw session usage across graph mixins (Round 5 Phase 1)

Convert 27 raw _get_session() calls to _execute_query() and _execute_chain_rebuild() across 6 graph mixin files:
- entity_ops.py: 6 methods converted
- video_ops.py: 3 methods + 1 chain rebuild
- frame_ops.py: 2 methods + 1 chain rebuild
- audio_ops.py: 3 methods + 1 chain rebuild
- relation_ops.py: 4 methods converted
- community_ops.py: 6 methods converted

Add _execute_chain_rebuild() helper to GraphNodeRepository for atomic
delete+create chain operations (scene, frame, segment rebuilds).

Update test mocks to assert against exec_fn instead of session.run.

Raw session calls reduced from 31 to 4 (87% reduction in mixin files).
Remaining 4 in agent_queries.py kept intentionally (complex multi-query logic).

All 1196 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: P0 DB cleanup — remove dead TS migrations, wire platform seed

P0-1 through P0-7 are all complete. Summary of what was done:

- P0-1: enterprise_members already in init.sql (verified)
- P0-2 to P0-5: SQL migration files 005-008 already existed in backend/migrations/
- P0-6: render-start.sh already used migrate.js (verified, old comment cleaned up)
- P0-7: 01_platform_required.sql already existed; this commit wires it into render-start.sh

This commit:
- DELETED: enterpriseMigration.ts, tier2Migration.ts, tier3Migration.ts, tier4Migration.ts
  (dead code — no longer called from anywhere; 005-008 SQL migrations cover their schema)
- FIXED: seed-demo-data.ts warning message now points to migrate.js (not old TS files)
- ADDED: render-start.sh Step 1b — loads docker/seeds/01_platform_required.sql after
  migrate.js, making the deployment self-contained (admin user + settings + permissions
  guaranteed on every deploy, idempotent via ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING)
- UPDATED: docker/seeds/README.md — reflects actual 4-step render-start.sh flow

No functionality changed. This is pure cleanup + startup reliability improvement.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: version check y fallback de certificado para PS 3.0/4.0

- Añade verificacion de version al inicio: PS 3.0 minimo requerido,
  con mensaje de error y enlace a WMF 5.1 si la version es inferior
- New-SelfSignedCertificate: parametros avanzados solo en PS 5.0+,
  fallback a sintaxis basica para Server 2012 / 2012 R2 (PS 3.0/4.0)

Compatibilidad resultante:
  PS 3.0 (Server 2012)       - HTTP + HTTPS (cert basico)
  PS 4.0 (Server 2012 R2)    - HTTP + HTTPS (cert basico)
  PS 5.1 (Server 2016+)      - HTTP + HTTPS (cert completo, recomendado)
  PS 7.x                     - HTTP + HTTPS (cert completo, recomendado)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: touch input via ESPHome I2C + BT speaker crack

Touch: LovyanGFX's lgfx::i2c fails after ESPHome's I2C bus recovery
(bus handles invalidated). Switch to ESPHome's native I2C by making
WinampDisplay inherit from i2c::I2CDevice. Uses read_register16()
for GT911's 16-bit register addresses (0x814E status, 0x8150 coords).
GT911 address probed at setup (0x14 or 0x5D).

BT crack: Write 960 bytes of silence to I2S1 after channel enable
to prevent pop/crack when BT speaker bridge starts receiving signal.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update README.md

commit.message:
[iOS] Fix ObjectDisposedException in ContentView.RemoveContentMask (#34680)

## Description

Fixes an `ObjectDisposedException` crash in
`ContentView.RemoveContentMask()` on iOS/MacCatalyst when the underlying
`CAShapeLayer` (`_contentMask`) has already been deallocated by the
native runtime during view hierarchy teardown.

### The Problem

During view lifecycle teardown, `WillRemoveSubview()` calls
`RemoveContentMask()`, which calls `RemoveFromSuperLayer()` on
`_contentMask`. When iOS has already collected the native
`CAShapeLayer`, the managed wrapper's `Handle` is `IntPtr.Zero`, calling
any method on it throws `ObjectDisposedException`.

This is a manifestation of the long-standing iOS binding lifecycle issue
documented in
[dotnet/macios#10562](https://github.com/dotnet/macios/issues/10562),
where `dealloc` of a UIView calls `RemoveFromSuperview()` for every
child while the native object is being deallocated, causing managed code
to interact with already-disposed native objects.

**Crash stack:**
```
ObjectDisposedException: Cannot access a disposed object.
Object name: 'Microsoft.Maui.Platform.StaticCAShapeLayer'.
   at ObjCRuntime.NativeObjectExtensions.GetNonNullHandle(INativeObject, String)
   at CoreAnimation.CALayer.RemoveFromSuperLayer()
   at Microsoft.Maui.Platform.ContentView.RemoveContentMask()
   at Microsoft.Maui.Platform.ContentView.WillRemoveSubview(UIView)
```

### The Fix

Added a disposal guard in `RemoveContentMask()` checking
`_contentMask.Handle != IntPtr.Zero` before calling
`RemoveFromSuperLayer()`. This follows the established pattern used
throughout the codebase in `ViewRenderer.cs`, `LayoutFactory2.cs`,
`CellRenderer.cs`, `SkiaGraphicsView.cs`, etc.

### Related Work

- dotnet/macios#10562 (upstream iOS binding lifecycle issue)
- #30861, #33818 (prior iOS disposal safety fixes in MAUI)

### Testing

Added device test `RemoveContentMaskDoesNotThrowWhenDisposed` that:
1. Creates a `ContentView` with an active clip mask
2. Asserts the mask was created as a `CAShapeLayer`
3. Disposes the native handle (simulating iOS deallocation) and asserts
`Handle == IntPtr.Zero`
4. Verifies `RemoveFromSuperview()` does not throw

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(maglev): add missing JMP to skip slow path in i32-range arithmetic

The i32-range optimization (d94caf2d) had a fall-through bug: after
JO slow_label, the fast path fell through directly into the slow path
code instead of jumping to done_label. This meant every i32-range
arithmetic operation was unconditionally calling the full runtime stub,
negating the optimization entirely.

Add JMP done_label after each JO/JCC check in the i32 fast path for
GenericAdd/Sub/Mul/Inc/Dec/Negate.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Certificate Farm Extension — 4 new farm cert types + movement/herd details

- backend/CertificateService.ts: Added 4 new farm cert types (movement_permit,
  herd_health_certificate, slaughter_fitness, export_health_certificate) to VALID_CERT_TYPES;
  added movementDetails + herdDetails to CertificateData interface, create/update INSERT,
  mapRow/mapListRow
- backend/database.ts: ALTER TABLE safety net for movement_details + herd_details JSONB columns;
  CREATE TABLE safety net updated to include both columns
- docker/init.sql: Canonical schema updated with movement_details JSONB + herd_details JSONB
- CertificateWriter.tsx: CERT_TYPES_FARM expanded with 4 new types; enterprise animal loading
  (when enterprise selected + farm cert type, loads animals by enterpriseId not ownerId);
  movement detail fields UI (fromLocation, toLocation, vehicleNumber, transportDate, driverName,
  purpose); herd detail fields UI (groupName, animalCount, species, purpose); isHerd certs
  allow optional animal selection; edit mode restores movement/herd details
- CertificatePrintView.tsx: movementDetails + herdDetails interfaces; movement section and
  herd section render in printed certificates
- VetCertificates.tsx: 4 new cert types added to CERT_TYPES filter list
- All 5 locale files: 4 new certType labels + movement/herd field labels + optionalForHerd key

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: viz flicker, touch input, and IP display

- Viz flicker: Draw visualizer panel into canvas sprite instead of
  separate sprite push. Eliminates double-write to same screen region
  (canvas push + viz push) that caused visible flashing.

- Touch not working: LovyanGFX tft_.getTouch() fails because ESPHome
  owns the I2C bus (bus acquisition error during init). Replace with
  direct GT911 register reads via lgfx::i2c — reads both home button
  (bit 4) and coordinate touches (0x8150-0x8153) in one pass.

- IP address 0: WiFi.localIP() doesn't work in ESPHome's WiFi
  management context. Use network::get_ip_addresses()[0].str_to()
  from ESPHome's network component instead.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(Link): remove validity bypass that renders invalid coordinates (#546)

`Link.svelte` had `{#if d.valid || true}` — the `|| true` made the guard
a no-op, rendering all links unconditionally. When coordinates are NaN
(e.g. during hierarchy layout transitions), d3 produces invalid SVG
paths, flooding the console with `Expected number` errors.

## Change

- `Link.svelte:187`: `{#if d.valid || true}` → `{#if d.valid}`, matching
the guard pattern already used by `Dot` and `Text` marks.

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: gka <617518+gka@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add export intervals feature

Add ability to export computed interval representations from graph-checker
to be used in the interval creator (index.html).

Features:
- Export button appears only when graph is validated as interval graph
- Transforms graph-checker format to index.html compatible JSON format
- Downloads intervals with node names (A, B, C, etc.) and colors
- Includes comprehensive Playwright test suite (9 tests total)

Implementation:
- Added lastValidIntervals property to store computed intervals
- Added exportIntervals() method for data transformation and download
- Added setupExportButton() to wire up event listener
- Store/clear intervals based on validation results

Tests:
- Export button visibility (appears/hidden based on graph validity)
- Interval storage (valid/invalid/empty graph scenarios)
- E2E export and import workflow verification
- All 9 tests passing

commit.message:
perf(maglev): skip Smi checks for i32-range operands in inline arithmetic

When both inputs to GenericAdd/Sub/Mul or the input to Inc/Dec/Negate
are in the i32_range set (computed by range analysis), skip the expensive
MOVSXD+CMP Smi-check pairs and use ADD/SUB/IMUL + JO overflow check
directly. This cuts ~6-8 instructions per arithmetic op in tight loops
where all operands are provably i32 (promoted globals, loop counters).

Same optimization applied to BitwiseOr/And/Xor: when both inputs are
i32-range, the slow-path code is elided entirely since bitwise ops on
i32 values cannot overflow.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
